people.biographical_detail: President of the ACC, organized the first camp. Surveyor by profession. Led scientific observations of Yoho Glacier. Chairman of Camp Committee.

articles_authored.title: The Canadian Rockies, a Field for an Alpine Club
articles_authored.emerald_lake_content: Lists Emerald Lake among turquoise-green lakes of the Main range, describes its setting amid forests and peaks.

people.biographical_detail: Writer and publicist. Authored the richest narrative account of the 1906 camp. His article is the primary source for camp life details.

articles_authored.title: Canada's First Alpine Club Camp
articles_authored.emerald_lake_content: Describes arrival at Emerald Lake Chalet, crossing the glacial delta on northern shore, ascending cliff wall. Notes transcendent beauty of lake at base of Mt. Burgess. Describes Emerald glacier trip, camp fire scene.

fonds_leads: Archives of Ontario — Frank Yeigh papers?

people.biographical_detail: Secretary of the ACC. Key founding figure. Address: 160 Furby Street, Winnipeg, Man. Wrote philosophical manifesto for the club.

people.biographical_detail: Co-authored glacier observations with brother George. Pioneered metal plate method for measuring glacier flow on the Illecillewaet.

Presbyterian minister and bestselling novelist writing as 'Ralph Connor'. Describes himself as 'the Missionary' in his Cascade article. Climbed Cascade Mountain in September 1891 — before the ACC existed.

people.biographical_detail: Camp photographer. Made solo ascent of Mt. Stephen in October 1904 — described as remarkable. Assisted on multiple official climbs of Vice-President. Later famous for 1909 Mt. Robson attempt.

people.biographical_detail: Botanist and author. Detailed the wildflowers of the Rockies and Selkirks. Notes Yellow Lady's Slipper (Cypripedium pubescens) growing on moraine from Emerald Lake to Yoho Valley in July.

articles_authored.title: The Mountain Wildflowers of Western Canada
articles_authored.emerald_lake_content: Describes Yellow Lady's Slipper orchids growing in thick clumps on a long moraine stretching from Emerald Lake to the foot of the Yoho Valley, blooming in July.

articles_authored.emerald_lake_content: Describes Emerald glacier trips and Burgess trail views of Emerald Lake and President range.

Presbyterian minister from Calgary. 'Born mountaineer' per Yeigh. Pioneered delta crossing. Led Emerald Glacier trips and Mt. Field ascent. Also authored Ascent of Mt. Macoun.

people.biographical_detail: From Warlingham, England. Climbed Mt. Collie on July 12 by different route from Patterson, meeting on summit. Was NOT visiting the camp — climbed independently with guide Christian Kaufmann.
Swiss guide who accompanied Henrietta Tuzo on her independent ascent of Mt. Collie July 12.
Professional photographer from Banff. Graduated to active membership July 13. Multiple photos credited in the journal.

people.biographical_detail: Graduated July 12. Photographer credited for Twin Falls Creek crossing photo.
photos_credited: CROSSING TWIN FALLS CREEK—YOHO VALLEY TRAIL

people.biographical_detail: Chinese cook from the CPR Yoho camps. Called 'Ping-Pong' by campers. Yeigh: 'May his fat shade never grow less.'
From Winnipeg. Graduated July 13. Singled out by Yeigh for wit and artistic talent at evening entertainments.
From Toronto. One of first two women to graduate at the camp (July 10). Singled out by Yeigh.
From Revelstoke. One of first two women to graduate at the camp (July 10).
Honorary President and Patron of the ACC. First called attention to the Rockies as a field for alpine work in his 1884 book 'England and Canada'. Engineer of the CPR.
CPR Second Vice-President. Wrote Greetings for the journal. Mentioned by Parker as associate member who would deplore defacement of mountain beauty.
Experienced mountaineer, assisted on July 11 official climb.
Daughter of Elizabeth Parker? Graduated July 12. Also climbed Wapta July 13.

items.title: Published manuscripts
items.source: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies

people.biographical_detail: Professor at Columbia University. First life member of the ACC. Bold pioneer per Parker. Authored Ascent of Mt. Hungabee.
Pioneering woman mountaineer. Authored Ascent of Mt. Assiniboine. Later became one of the most-travelled women of her era.
Authored First Ascent of Central Peak of Mt. Bagheera.

articles_authored.title: The First Ascent of Central Peak of Mt. Bagheera

people.biographical_detail: Authored Ascent of Mt. Hermit.
President of the American Alpine Club. Honorary member of ACC. Visited Selkirks in 1890, brought many others. Drove formation of AAC Alpine section. Authored Ascent of Mt. Goodsir.
Of Matterhorn fame. Named The Vice-President and The President. Visited Canadian Rockies 1901 with four Swiss guides. Honorary member ACC.
Explored the Rockies 1897-1902. Climbed extensively in the Blaeberry area. Co-authored 'Climbs and Explorations in the Canadian Rockies.' Honorary member ACC.
Captured Mt. Assiniboine 1901. Made 'big killing' 1902: Columbia, Bryce, Lyall, Alexandra. Named Angle Peak on VP ridge. Author of 'In the Heart of the Canadian Rockies.' Honorary member ACC.

sources: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
